heatindexbr: Heat Index Mapping for the Brazilian Semiarid Region

Source: R/heatindexbr-package.R
heatindexbr-package.Rd

The heatindexbr package provides pre-computed Heat Index (HI) rasters for the Brazilian Semiarid region (1,477 municipalities) at four synoptic hours (00h, 09h, 15h, 21h local time) and tools for spatial extraction.

Main functions

  • hi_download: Download rasters from Zenodo repository

  • hi_municipality: Extract HI for municipalities by name or code

  • hi_shape: Extract HI for any area (shapefile or sf object)

  • hi_search: Search for valid municipality names

  • hi_plot: Generate standardized HI maps

Data

Rasters are interpolated using External Drift Kriging with altitude (SRTM) and ERA5-Land temperature as covariates, validated against INMET automatic weather station data (2025) with LOOCV R² = 0.884 at 15h local time.

Citation

Campos Neto, A. et al. (2026). Heat Index mapping for the Brazilian Semiarid: spatial interpolation under sparse monitoring networks. International Journal of Climatology. (in review)

See also

Useful links:

  • https://github.com/antcamposneto/heatindexbr

  • doi:10.5281/zenodo.20942619

  • https://antcneto.github.io/heatindexbr/
